title: neohymenopogon assamicus
intro: Neohymenopogon assamicus, a flowering plant species native to the northeastern Indian states, particularly Assam. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses or graminoids. The medicinal properties of Neohymenopogon assamicus are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its herbal preparations include decoctions, infusions, and extracts that can be consumed orally to reap its health benefits.
